reasoning: No direct relationship between V and I or R is evident from the initial observation. Increasing or decreasing I or R might provide more insight.
The value of V has increased when I was increased but R remained constant. This suggests that V might depend on I in some way, possibly linearly since V increases proportionally with I.

Observing that V increases as I increases, and R remains constant, suggests a possible linear relationship between V and I. The observed data points (I=2, V=20), (I=3, V=30), and (I=4, V=40) indicate that for every unit increase in I, V increases by 10 units. Therefore, V might be directly proportional to I.
